name: Metrics Model: OSS Project Viability: Strategy
description: A focused metrics guide exploring how to assess risk and project direction by observing structural indicators of organizational influence.
author: CHAOSS Project
abstract: This metrics model details specific mechanisms for analyzing an open-source project's long-term corporate and organizational dependencies. It profiles analytical methods across structural indicators, including Programming Language Distribution, Bus Factor, Elephant Factor, and Organizational Influence trends.
audience: OSPO managers, system architects, software dependency compliance officers
learningObjectives:
After this lesson, the learner should be able to:
- Distinguish between Bus Factor and Elephant Factor as organizational risk indicators
- Interpret Programming Language Distribution as a strategic dependency signal
- Apply the OSS Project Viability: Strategy model to assess organizational concentration risk
- Use CHAOSS metrics to support institutional open source adoption decisions
teaches: Interpret organizational influence patterns and software distributions to identify dependency risks for your institution.
competencyRequired: Knowledge of software supply chain concepts and institutional risk tolerance modeling
